question: What do we call the properties or things a robot object knows about itself in Python?
What does the keyword 'self' stand for inside a Python class?

options.A: It means 'this specific robot object' so it can keep track of its own unique data
options.B: It means the code should stop running and turn itself off
options.C: It is a command used to print things to the screen
options.D: It forces all robots to have the exact same name

question: Inside a class definition, which special method block is used to set up or initialize a robot's attributes when it is first built?

Look at this code snippet:

class Robot:
    def __init__(self, name):
        self.name = name

robot1 = Robot("Huey")
robot2 = Robot("Scorpion")

What will print if you type 'print(robot2.name)'?
If you have a robot object named 'my_bot', how do you access or read its 'battery_level' attribute?
